THE smell hits first. In a district hospital just outside Dhaka, a patient’s family member searches for a working toilet. Brick economy behind air crisis

AIR pollution crisis is often framed through traffic congestion, seasonal smog or industrial emissions. Yet, one of its most persistent and structurally embedded drivers lies in the country’s construction economy. As urban expansion accelerates and infrastructure demand rises, fired clay bricks remain the dominant building material. This dependence has turned brick......

Managing tides in south-west

A TRAGEDY is quietly unfolding in Bangladesh’s south-west. The Water Development Board has for decades treated the rivers like clogged pipes that need ‘cleaning’ through expensive structural engineering model, including mechanical dredging. Yet, as any resident near the Kobadak or Hari rivers will say, the silt returns almost as fast as the machines can remove it......
